terms.definition: Severe systemic IgE-mediated hypersensitivity reaction with airway, breathing or circulatory compromise.
terms.exampleSentence: Intramuscular adrenaline was given immediately for peanut-induced anaphylaxis.
terms.contextNotes: First-line is IM adrenaline 500 micrograms in adults; observe 6-12 hours for biphasic reactions.

terms.definition: Endogenous catecholamine and emergency drug acting on alpha and beta adrenergic receptors.
terms.exampleSentence: She received 500 micrograms of intramuscular adrenaline for her anaphylactic reaction.
terms.contextNotes: 1 mg IV every 3-5 minutes during ALS cardiac arrest algorithm.

terms.definition: Standardised neurological scoring system grading eye, verbal and motor responses out of 15.
terms.exampleSentence: Her Glasgow Coma Scale dropped to 7, prompting urgent intubation.
terms.contextNotes: GCS ≤8 is generally an indication for definitive airway protection.

terms.definition: Sudden cessation of effective cardiac mechanical activity confirmed by absent central pulse.
terms.exampleSentence: CPR was commenced immediately when the patient suffered an in-hospital cardiac arrest.
terms.contextNotes: Shockable rhythms (VF/pVT) require defibrillation as soon as possible.

terms.definition: Circulatory failure due to reduced intravascular volume from haemorrhage or fluid loss.
terms.exampleSentence: The trauma patient with massive haemorrhage was in class III hypovolaemic shock.
terms.contextNotes: Treat with prompt fluid and early blood products in haemorrhage.

terms.definition: Insertion of an endotracheal tube to secure and ventilate the airway.
terms.exampleSentence: Rapid sequence intubation was performed for airway protection in the unconscious trauma patient.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm tube position with capnography and chest auscultation.

terms.definition: Therapeutic delivery of a controlled electric shock to terminate ventricular fibrillation or pulseless VT.
terms.exampleSentence: A single biphasic shock at 200 joules restored sinus rhythm during defibrillation.
terms.contextNotes: Resume CPR for two minutes immediately after each shock.

terms.definition: Competitive opioid receptor antagonist used to reverse opioid-induced respiratory depression.
terms.exampleSentence: Naloxone 400 micrograms IV was administered for suspected opioid overdose.
terms.contextNotes: Short half-life requires repeat dosing or infusion in long-acting opioid overdose.

terms.definition: Hospital protocol activating a multidisciplinary team for the immediate care of a major trauma patient.
terms.exampleSentence: A trauma call was put out for the motorcyclist with suspected pelvic injury.
terms.contextNotes: ATLS principles guide the structured ABCDE primary survey.

terms.definition: Process of prioritising patients for assessment and treatment based on clinical urgency.
terms.exampleSentence: On triage she was assigned a category 2 due to chest pain and tachycardia.
terms.contextNotes: Manchester Triage System uses five colour-coded categories.

terms.definition: Acute viral lower respiratory infection of small airways in infants, usually caused by RSV.
terms.exampleSentence: The wheezy 4-month-old with coryza and crackles was admitted with bronchiolitis.
terms.contextNotes: Care is supportive; bronchodilators and steroids are not routinely effective.

terms.definition: Viral laryngotracheobronchitis in young children causing barking cough and inspiratory stridor.
terms.exampleSentence: Single-dose oral dexamethasone was given for moderate croup with stridor at rest.
terms.contextNotes: Nebulised adrenaline for severe respiratory distress in addition to steroid.

terms.definition: Acute vasculitis of childhood causing fever, rash, conjunctivitis and a risk of coronary artery aneurysms.
terms.exampleSentence: Persistent fever, strawberry tongue and red palms led to a diagnosis of Kawasaki disease.
terms.contextNotes: Treated with IV immunoglobulin and high-dose aspirin to reduce coronary aneurysm risk.

terms.definition: Generalised seizure occurring in a child aged 6 months to 5 years with fever, without intracranial infection.
terms.exampleSentence: His brief generalised febrile convulsion settled before reaching the emergency department.
terms.contextNotes: Most do not require investigation if simple and child fully recovers.

terms.definition: Widely used analgesic and antipyretic acting centrally; hepatotoxic in overdose.
terms.exampleSentence: Weight-based paracetamol was given orally for the child's fever and discomfort.
terms.contextNotes: N-acetylcysteine reverses hepatotoxicity if given within 8-10 hours.

terms.definition: Yellow discolouration of skin and sclerae in newborns due to elevated bilirubin.
terms.exampleSentence: Phototherapy was commenced for significant neonatal jaundice on day 3.
terms.contextNotes: Jaundice in the first 24 hours of life is always pathological.

terms.definition: Bedside scoring tool aggregating physiological observations in children to flag clinical deterioration.
terms.exampleSentence: The rising paediatric early warning score prompted senior review and escalation.
terms.contextNotes: Triggers escalation pathways for paediatric inpatients.

terms.definition: Administration of an antigenic preparation to stimulate protective immunity against an infectious disease.
terms.exampleSentence: His routine childhood vaccinations were up to date according to the red book.
terms.contextNotes: Refer to the UK Green Book for the current schedule and contraindications.

terms.definition: Malignant neoplasm of breast tissue, most often invasive ductal carcinoma.
terms.exampleSentence: A new lump on self-examination led to a diagnosis of early-stage breast cancer.
terms.contextNotes: Triple assessment: clinical, imaging and biopsy.

terms.definition: Use of cytotoxic drugs to treat cancer by inhibiting proliferation of malignant cells.
terms.exampleSentence: Adjuvant chemotherapy was offered after surgical resection of the colorectal tumour.
terms.contextNotes: Patients with fever during chemo cycles need urgent neutropenic sepsis pathway.

terms.definition: Spread of malignant cells from a primary tumour to a distant non-contiguous site.
terms.exampleSentence: Liver metastases were detected on staging CT in this patient with colorectal cancer.
terms.contextNotes: Lung, liver, bone and brain are the most common metastatic sites.

terms.definition: Life-threatening sepsis in a patient with neutrophils <0.5 × 10⁹/L, typically post-chemotherapy.
terms.exampleSentence: Empirical piperacillin-tazobactam was started within an hour for suspected neutropenic sepsis.
terms.contextNotes: Treat as medical emergency: antibiotics within one hour of presentation.

terms.definition: Specialist care that improves quality of life for patients with serious or terminal illness.
terms.exampleSentence: She was referred to community palliative care for symptom control in advanced lung cancer.
terms.contextNotes: Focuses on holistic symptom management, not active life prolongation.

terms.definition: Strong opioid analgesic used for severe acute or cancer-related pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Subcutaneous morphine via a syringe driver controlled her end-of-life pain.
terms.contextNotes: Anticipate constipation and prescribe a regular laxative.

terms.definition: Low-dose X-ray imaging of the breast used in screening and diagnostic assessment.
terms.exampleSentence: Routine mammography detected a small spiculated lesion in the upper outer quadrant.
terms.contextNotes: UK NHS Breast Screening offers mammography every 3 years from age 50-71.
